PaperExtraction.schema_version: Schema version string for this CoralSIE extraction format.

bibliography.source_file_path: Input Markdown or PDF path supplied to the extractor.
bibliography.ref_id: Manually supplied RefID associated with this paper.
bibliography.title: Paper title.

PaperExtraction.ocean: Ocean basin location where the study took place.
PaperExtraction.study_location: Specific location where the study took place, e.g., name of bay, reef, island, and country.
PaperExtraction.study_site: Categorical variable for whether study was in a field or laboratory. (eg: field, laboratory, field and laboratory)
PaperExtraction.study_type: Categorical variable for manipulative or observational experiment. (eg: manipulative, observational, manipulative and observational)
PaperExtraction.event_latitude: If there was a single stressor event, as with dredging or mining, etc., the location in latitude.
PaperExtraction.event_longitude: If there was a single stressor event, as with dredging or mining, etc., the location in longitude.
PaperExtraction.num_setups: Number of setups recorded in the paper, where one setup is a unique set of coral genus and species, sediment composition, sediment level, and sediment exposure duration. This should be the length of the setups list.
PaperExtraction.num_species: The number of different species (<genus> <species>) that are examined in this paper.
PaperExtraction.num_responses: number of response outcome types recorded in paper. This should be the length of the "responses" list in each setup. Each setup should have the same set of responses.

population.coral_age_class: Categorical variable describing the broad age class of the coral used in the focal reference. (eg: gametes, larva, juvenile, adult)
population.colony_form: Categorical variable for the morphological form that the coral colony takes. (eg: massive, palmate, plating, ramose, branching, submassive, cup, encrusting, flabellate, foliaceous, free-living, phaceloid, etc)

exposure.sediment_composition: Brief description of substrate type and size, if analyzed; note if sediment was dried.
exposure.sediment_level: Numeric value describing level of exposure to sediment; if a range is reported instead of a mean, report the midpoint of the range here.
exposure.sediment_level_uom: Unit of measurement corresponding to sediment level, most often mg/L or NTU for suspended sediment or mg/cm2/day for deposited sediment.
exposure.sediment_level_mg_cm2_day: Sediment level converted to mg/cm2/day; if already in this unit, repeat the sediment level here.
exposure.sediment_level_mg_l: Sediment level converted to mg/L; if already in this unit, repeat the sediment level here.
exposure.sediment_level_average_type: Categorical variable for type of average reported for the sediment level. (eg: absolute, mean, median, mode, range, etc)
exposure.sediment_n_for_computing_average: Sample size used for computing average of sediment level; if N is reported as a range, use the lower end and note the reported range in notes.
exposure.sediment_n_uom: Unit of measurement corresponding to sediment N. (eg: sediment traps for deposited sediment, etc)
exposure.lower_error: Lower estimate of error for reported sediment level, not the lower bound.
exposure.upper_error: Upper estimate of error for reported sediment level, not the upper bound.
exposure.error_type: Type of error for reported sediment level. (eg: s.e., s.d., 95% CI)
exposure.exposure_duration: Numeric value of duration of time that coral was exposed to sediment level.
exposure.duration_unit: Unit of measurement corresponding to exposure duration.
exposure.duration_days: Numeric value of duration of time that coral was exposed to sediment level in days.
exposure.notes: Any commentary concerning sediment stressor, including figure, table, or text from which information was taken.

binary_responses.high_yield_chla: Whether this condition/treatment/control found increased yield or chlorophyll A content with respect to control/ambient conditions. Leave blank if not reported or mentioned. (eg: 0, 1)
binary_responses.reduced_p_r: Whether this condition/treatment/control found reduced photosynthesis/respiration ratios with respect to control/ambient conditions. (eg: 0, 1)
binary_responses.reduced_photosynthetic_efficiency: Whether this condition/treatment/control found reduced photosynthetic efficiency with respect to control/ambient conditions. (eg: 0, 1)
binary_responses.reduced_growth_rate: Whether this condition/treatment/control found reduced growth rate with respect to control/ambient conditions. (eg: 0, 1)
binary_responses.death_of_colonies: Whether this condition/treatment/control found death of whole colonies with respect to control/ambient conditions. (eg: 0, 1)
binary_responses.binary_response_source: Notes on where binary data were derived.

data_reported.non_adverse_effect_reported: Whether the study reported binary data for non-adverse effects of sediment on coral under specified condition/treatment. (eg: 0, 1)
data_reported.adverse_effect_reported: Whether the study reported binary data for adverse effects of sediment on coral under specified condition/treatment. (eg: 0, 1)
data_reported.mortality_reported: Whether the study reported binary data for mortality of any coral age class, partial or total, under specified condition/treatment. (eg: 0, 1)
data_reported.adult_mortality_reported: Whether the study reported binary data for mortality of adult corals only, partial or total, under specified condition/treatment. (eg: 0, 1)

evidence.source_quote: Short quote or compact evidence span supporting this setup.
evidence.source_page: Page number where the supporting setup evidence appears, if available.
evidence.source_table_or_figure: Table, figure, appendix, or section identifier for the supporting setup evidence.

responses.response_type: Categorical variable that broadly describes the measured coral response. Suggested list is not exhaustive. (eg: Bleaching, Calcification rate, Chlorophyll concentration, Growth rate, Mortality rate, Mucus Production, Photosynthesis, Respiration, etc)
responses.level: Numeric value describing coral's response to condition/treatment.
responses.unit: Unit of measurement corresponding to response level.
responses.level_type: Categorical variable for type of average reported for the response. (eg: absolute/raw, mean, median, mode, range, etc.)
responses.sample_size_n: Sample size used for computing average of response; if N is reported as a range, use the lower end and note the reported range in response_source.
responses.n_unit: Unit of measurement corresponding to response N. (eg: colonies, fragments, branches, etc)
responses.time_to_response_numeric: Numeric value indicating amount of time elapsed between start of stressor and start of response.
responses.time_to_response_uom: Unit of measurement corresponding to amount of time elapsed between start of stressor and start of response.
responses.response_source: Figure, table, or location in text from which these data were found, plus any other notes on this response.

evidence.source_quote: Short quote or compact evidence span supporting this response.
evidence.source_page: Page number where the supporting response evidence appears, if available.
evidence.source_table_or_figure: Table, figure, appendix, or section identifier for the supporting response evidence.

PaperExtraction.warnings: List of extraction warnings or uncertainty notes.
